# guild CLI reference

Every `guild <verb>` subcommand generated from the live cobra tree.

## Verbs

- [`guild hints`](#guild-hints) — hint engine inspection + administration
- [`guild hints disable`](#guild-hints-disable) — disable a rule
- [`guild hints enable`](#guild-hints-enable) — re-enable a previously disabled rule
- [`guild hints list`](#guild-hints-list) — list all registered hint rules with severity and enabled state
- [`guild hints prune`](#guild-hints-prune) — run the auto-prune pass (demote/disable rules below 14.46% hit rate)
- [`guild hints stats`](#guild-hints-stats) — per-rule fire count, scored count, hit rate, and prune-floor comparison
- [`guild init`](#guild-init) — scaffold AGENTS.md and register this repo with guild
- [`guild lore`](#guild-lore) — knowledge lifecycle (read/write/decay/supersede)
- [`guild lore appraise`](#guild-lore-appraise) — hybrid search (BM25 + recency + title-boost)
- [`guild lore archive`](#guild-lore-archive) — write snapshot.json for git-trackable project checkpoint (alias: export)
- [`guild lore catalog`](#guild-lore-catalog) — bulk-import .md files as lore entries
- [`guild lore commune`](#guild-lore-commune) — health report for oath bloat and duplicate lore
- [`guild lore coverage-reconcile`](#guild-lore-coverage-reconcile) — reset vector_coverage_den to the live active-entry count
- [`guild lore dossier`](#guild-lore-dossier) — emit ~2k-token project context bundle for subagents
- [`guild lore echoes`](#guild-lore-echoes) — surface stale entries for review
- [`guild lore embed-rebuild`](#guild-lore-embed-rebuild) — reset and rebuild the embedding vector index for the active project
- [`guild lore health`](#guild-lore-health) — embedder health report (coverage, pending, stale, errors)
- [`guild lore init`](#guild-lore-init) — register the current git repository as a guild project
- [`guild lore inquest`](#guild-lore-inquest) — audit oath wall for >60-word principles
- [`guild lore inscribe`](#guild-lore-inscribe) — inscribe a new knowledge entry into the lore
- [`guild lore link`](#guild-lore-link) — create provenance link between entries (cross-project allowed)
- [`guild lore list`](#guild-lore-list) — browse entries with optional filters
- [`guild lore meld`](#guild-lore-meld) — find duplicate lore entries across projects
- [`guild lore oath`](#guild-lore-oath) — list behavioral principles
- [`guild lore reforge`](#guild-lore-reforge) — mark entry superseded by a newer one (atomic)
- [`guild lore restore`](#guild-lore-restore) — restore lore from snapshot.json (alias: import)
- [`guild lore ripples`](#guild-lore-ripples) — walk provenance edges from a seed entry
- [`guild lore seal`](#guild-lore-seal) — seal an entry, archiving it from active circulation
- [`guild lore study`](#guild-lore-study) — full detail + linked entries graph
- [`guild lore unlink`](#guild-lore-unlink) — remove a provenance link between entries
- [`guild lore update`](#guild-lore-update) — edit fields or reclassify an entry
- [`guild lore whispers`](#guild-lore-whispers) — show current idea pipeline (kind=idea, status=seed|exploring)
- [`guild mcp`](#guild-mcp) — MCP server subcommands
- [`guild mcp install`](#guild-mcp-install) — print the recommended MCP registration command for each detected agent client
- [`guild mcp serve`](#guild-mcp-serve) — run the guild MCP stdio server for AI agents
- [`guild quest`](#guild-quest) — task lifecycle (post/accept/journal/clear/coordinate)
- [`guild quest accept`](#guild-quest-accept) — atomically claim a quest
- [`guild quest active`](#guild-quest-active) — in-progress tasks across all projects
- [`guild quest archive`](#guild-quest-archive) — snapshot quest state to .guild/snapshot.json (opt-in)
- [`guild quest bounties`](#guild-quest-bounties) — session-start: oath + echoes + last brief + top task + parallelism
- [`guild quest brief`](#guild-quest-brief) — write session-end handoff for the next agent
- [`guild quest campaign`](#guild-quest-campaign) — bulk-set campaign
- [`guild quest campfire`](#guild-quest-campfire) — save working state before compaction
- [`guild quest forfeit`](#guild-quest-forfeit) — release a claim without completing
- [`guild quest fulfill`](#guild-quest-fulfill) — fulfill a quest (cascades unblock)
- [`guild quest guild`](#guild-quest-guild) — per-project summary by campaign
- [`guild quest init`](#guild-quest-init) — register this repo as a guild project
- [`guild quest journal`](#guild-quest-journal) — append a task-scoped journal note
- [`guild quest list`](#guild-quest-list) — list open quests
- [`guild quest orders`](#guild-quest-orders) — show quests assigned to an agent
- [`guild quest post`](#guild-quest-post) — create a new quest
- [`guild quest pulse`](#guild-quest-pulse) — quest quality dashboard: rework, churn, hot files
- [`guild quest restore`](#guild-quest-restore) — restore quest state from .guild/snapshot.json
- [`guild quest scroll`](#guild-quest-scroll) — full history: status + journal + timeline
- [`guild quest search`](#guild-quest-search) — search quests by keyword or semantic paraphrase
- [`guild quest summon`](#guild-quest-summon) — delegate a quest to a teammate agent
- [`guild quest update`](#guild-quest-update) — modify a quest's spec after post
- [`guild status`](#guild-status) — dashboard: last briefing, oath, top bounty, parallelism (alias of quest bounties)
- [`guild telemetry`](#guild-telemetry) — telemetry analytics (usage log, token estimates)
- [`guild telemetry tokens`](#guild-telemetry-tokens) — estimate token cost from usage.log response bytes
- [`guild version`](#guild-version) — print guild version information

## `guild hints`

hint engine inspection + administration

**Usage**

```
guild hints
```

Inspect and administer the SQL-backed hint engine (QUEST-58).

The engine fires advisory lines on top of tool responses. The launch-9
rules were calibrated in ENTRY-29; later additions (e.g. the thin-citation
rule from QUEST-167) ship seeded via migrations alongside the rest. Use
these subcommands to see rule state, compute per-rule hit rates, prune
under-performing rules, or manually override enable/disable.

## `guild hints disable`

disable a rule

**Usage**

```
guild hints disable RULE_ID
```

## `guild hints enable`

re-enable a previously disabled rule

**Usage**

```
guild hints enable RULE_ID
```

## `guild hints list`

list all registered hint rules with severity and enabled state

**Usage**

```
guild hints list
```

## `guild hints prune`

run the auto-prune pass (demote/disable rules below 14.46% hit rate)

**Usage**

```
guild hints prune
```

## `guild hints stats`

per-rule fire count, scored count, hit rate, and prune-floor comparison

**Usage**

```
guild hints stats
```

## `guild init`

scaffold AGENTS.md and register this repo with guild

**Usage**

```
guild init [flags]
```

guild init — per-project scaffold

Run inside a git repository. Detects the project name, shows a plan
(register in ~/.guild/, create-or-merge AGENTS.md, MCP registration hint),
then prompts [Y/n] before acting. Accepts all defaults without prompting
when stdin is not a TTY (piped / CI invocation).

Flags:
  --yes             accept all defaults, no prompts
  --dry-run         show the plan without executing any changes
  --print-agents-md emit only the AGENTS.md snippet to stdout (for piping)

**Flags**

| flag | type | default | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--dry-run` | bool | `false` | show what would happen without making changes |
| `--print-agents-md` | bool | `false` | emit only the AGENTS.md template snippet to stdout |
| `--yes` | bool | `false` | accept all defaults without prompting |

## `guild lore`

knowledge lifecycle (read/write/decay/supersede)

**Usage**

```
guild lore
```

## `guild lore appraise`

hybrid search (BM25 + recency + title-boost)

**Usage**

```
guild lore appraise QUERY [flags]
```

**Aliases:** check

**Flags**

| flag | type | default | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--all` | bool | `false` | include archived/superseded entries |
| `--all-projects` | bool | `true` | search across every project (default) |
| `--limit` | int | `10` | max results |
| `--project`, `-p` | string | `—` | project id (defaults to git toplevel) |
| `--since` | string | `—` | only entries created within: Nd\|Nw\|Nm |
| `--w-fts` | float64 | `0` | override scoring weight for BM25 (0..1) |
| `--w-recency` | float64 | `0` | override scoring weight for recency (0..1) |

```
guild lore archive [flags]
```

| `--output`, `-o` | string | `—` | output path (default: <repo>/.guild/snapshot.json) |
| `--project`, `-p` | string | `—` | project id (defaults to git toplevel) |

## `guild lore catalog`

bulk-import .md files as lore entries

**Usage**

```
guild lore catalog DIR [flags]
```

**Aliases:** migrate

Bulk-import .md files under DIR as lore entries. Idempotent on re-runs.

**Flags**

| flag | type | default | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--json` | bool | `false` | emit structured JSON result instead of formatted text |
| `--kind` | string | `—` | override kind |
| `--project`, `-p` | string | `—` | project override |
| `--tags` | string | `—` | comma-separated tags |
| `--topic` | string | `—` | override per-file topic; default: file stem |

## `guild lore commune`

health report for oath bloat and duplicate lore

**Usage**

```
guild lore commune [flags]
```

**Aliases:** lint

Health report for oath bloat and duplicate lore. fix=true auto-remediates severe issues.

**Flags**

| flag | type | default | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--all-projects` | bool | `false` | check across every project |
| `--fix` | bool | `false` | apply safe auto-remediation |
| `--json` | bool | `false` | emit structured JSON result instead of formatted text |
| `--project`, `-p` | string | `—` | project override |

## `guild lore coverage-reconcile`

reset vector_coverage_den to the live active-entry count

**Usage**

```
guild lore coverage-reconcile [flags]
```

**Aliases:** fix-coverage

Reset meta.vector_coverage_den to the live COUNT(*) WHERE status NOT IN ('archived','parked') and report before/after values. Corrects num > den drift that produces coverage > 100%. Backfill also runs this automatically, so this command is a manual escape hatch.

**Flags**

| flag | type | default | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--json` | bool | `false` | emit structured JSON result instead of formatted text |
| `--project`, `-p` | string | `—` | project override |

emit ~2k-token project context bundle for subagents

**Usage**

```
guild lore dossier [flags]
```

Compile ~2000-token project context for subagent spawn prompts. Use when delegating multi-file work.

**Flags**

| flag | type | default | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--json` | bool | `false` | emit structured JSON result instead of formatted text |
| `--project`, `-p` | string | `—` | project override |

## `guild lore echoes`

surface stale entries for review

**Usage**

```
guild lore echoes [flags]
```

**Aliases:** stale

List stale/decayed entries the next agent should review or reforge. Cross-checks file_path modification times when --git-aware is set.

**Flags**

| flag | type | default | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--git-aware` | bool | `false` | also flag entries whose file_path changed after creation |
| `--json` | bool | `false` | emit structured JSON result instead of formatted text |
| `--project`, `-p` | string | `—` | project override |

## `guild lore embed-rebuild`

reset and rebuild the embedding vector index for the active project

**Usage**

```
guild lore embed-rebuild [flags]
```

**Aliases:** embed-reset

Delete all lore_vectors rows for the active project, flip every active entry's vector_state to 'pending', then encode each entry and insert the resulting int8 vectors. Safe under concurrent MCP servers: uses BEGIN IMMEDIATE for the reset phase and INSERT OR IGNORE for each vector write (ADR-003 invariants).

**Flags**

| flag | type | default | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--json` | bool | `false` | emit structured JSON result instead of formatted text |
| `--project`, `-p` | string | `—` | project override |

## `guild lore health`

embedder health report (coverage, pending, stale, errors)

**Usage**

```
guild lore health [flags]
```

**Aliases:** embedder-health

Print the embedder health section: model_id, tokenizer_hash, runtime_version, dim, coverage (num/den and percent), pending count, stale count, last encode error (if any), last successful encode timestamp, and rolling embed_error_count.

**Flags**

| flag | type | default | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--json` | bool | `false` | emit structured JSON result instead of formatted text |
| `--project`, `-p` | string | `—` | project override |

## `guild lore inquest`

audit oath wall for >60-word principles

**Usage**

```
guild lore inquest [flags]
```

**Aliases:** audit

Audit the oath wall for narrative-bloat principles (>60 words). Demote bloated principles to decisions.

**Flags**

| flag | type | default | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--all-projects` | bool | `false` | scan every project |
| `--json` | bool | `false` | emit structured JSON result instead of formatted text |
| `--project`, `-p` | string | `—` | project override |

## `guild lore inscribe`

inscribe a new knowledge entry into the lore

**Usage**

```
guild lore inscribe TITLE... [flags]
```

**Aliases:** add

Store knowledge that transcends the current task — patterns, decisions, research that outlive the quest. Call lore_appraise first; pass informs=[IDs] for entries that informed this one to create provenance edges at write-time. Cross-project dedup and principle-hygiene warnings are built in.

**Flags**

| flag | type | default | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--informs` | stringArray | `[]` | source entry id (LORE-N, ENTRY-N, or bare N) that informs this entry — repeatable, creates provenance edges |
| `--json` | bool | `false` | emit structured JSON result instead of formatted text |
| `--kind`, `-k` | string | `—` | entry kind (required): idea\|research\|decision\|observation\|principle |
| `--needs-review` | bool | `false` | flag for human review |
| `--no-warn` | bool | `false` | suppress principle-bloat warning |
| `--project`, `-p` | string | `—` | project override |
| `--prompted-by` | string | `—` | QUEST-N that prompted this inscribe |
| `--source` | string | `—` | source URL or reference |
| `--strict-project` | bool | `false` | scope dedup to the current project only (default: cross-project) |
| `--summary`, `-s` | string | `—` | 2-3 sentence summary (required) |
| `--tags` | stringArray | `[]` | semantic tag (repeatable) |
| `--topic`, `-t` | string | `—` | topic slug (required) |

## `guild lore link`

create provenance link between entries (cross-project allowed)

**Usage**

```
guild lore link FROM_ID [flags]
```

Create an informs provenance edge between two entries. Cross-project allowed.

**Flags**

| flag | type | default | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--informs` | string | `—` | target entry id (LORE-N or bare N) — the one being informed |
| `--json` | bool | `false` | emit structured JSON result instead of formatted text |
| `--project`, `-p` | string | `—` | project override |
| `--relation` | string | `—` | link relation: informs (default) \| supersedes \| contradicts |

## `guild lore meld`

find duplicate lore entries across projects

**Usage**

```
guild lore meld [flags]
```

**Aliases:** dedupe

Find duplicate lore entries across projects. Surfaces reforge candidates. Cross-project by default.

**Flags**

| flag | type | default | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--all-projects` | bool | `false` | span every project |
| `--json` | bool | `false` | emit structured JSON result instead of formatted text |
| `--project`, `-p` | string | `—` | project override |
| `--threshold` | string | `—` | Jaccard near-match threshold (e.g. 0.7); omit for exact-only (default) |

## `guild lore reforge`

mark entry superseded by a newer one (atomic)

**Usage**

```
guild lore reforge OLD_ID [flags]
```

**Aliases:** supersede

Mark an older entry superseded by a newer one. Preserves history — do NOT delete.

**Flags**

| flag | type | default | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--json` | bool | `false` | emit structured JSON result instead of formatted text |
| `--project`, `-p` | string | `—` | project override |
| `--with` | string | `—` | the newer entry that replaces it (LORE-N or bare N) |

## `guild lore restore`

restore lore from snapshot.json (alias: import)

**Usage**

```
guild lore restore [flags]
```

**Aliases:** import

**Flags**

| flag | type | default | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |

## `guild lore ripples`

walk provenance edges from a seed entry

**Usage**

```
guild lore ripples ENTRY_ID [flags]
```

Walk the provenance graph from a seed entry via entry_links. direction=out follows descendants (what this informed), direction=in follows ancestors (what informed this), direction=both unions both. Default depth=3, max 10.

**Flags**

| flag | type | default | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--depth` | int | `0` | walk depth (default 3, max 10) |
| `--direction` | string | `—` | out\|in\|both (default out) |
| `--json` | bool | `false` | emit structured JSON result instead of formatted text |
| `--project`, `-p` | string | `—` | project override |
| `--relation` | string | `—` | informs\|supersedes\|contradicts\|all (default all) |

## `guild lore seal`

seal an entry, archiving it from active circulation

**Usage**

```
guild lore seal ENTRY_ID [flags]
```

Seal (archive) an entry from active circulation. History is preserved.

**Flags**

| flag | type | default | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--json` | bool | `false` | emit structured JSON result instead of formatted text |
| `--project`, `-p` | string | `—` | project override |


## `guild lore unlink`

remove a provenance link between entries

**Usage**

```
guild lore unlink FROM_ID [flags]
```

Remove an informs (or supersedes/contradicts) edge from the provenance graph. Idempotent: removing a non-existent edge returns success with a 'no matching edge' note.

**Flags**

| flag | type | default | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--informs` | string | `—` | target entry id (LORE-N or bare N), the one that was being informed |
| `--json` | bool | `false` | emit structured JSON result instead of formatted text |
| `--project`, `-p` | string | `—` | project override |
| `--relation` | string | `—` | link relation to remove: informs (default) \| supersedes \| contradicts |

## `guild mcp`

MCP server subcommands

**Usage**

```
guild mcp
```

## `guild mcp install`

print the recommended MCP registration command for each detected agent client

**Usage**

```
guild mcp install [flags]
```

guild mcp install — delegate MCP registration to each client's official CLI

Detects which MCP clients are present on this machine (Claude Code, Cursor,
Codex) and prints the recommended command to register guild with each:

  guild binary: /usr/local/bin/guild

  Detected agent clients:
    ✓ Claude Code
    ✗ Cursor  (not detected)

  Run the command for each agent you use:

    # Claude Code
    claude mcp add guild --scope user -- /usr/local/bin/guild mcp serve

guild never writes client config files directly — it delegates to each
client's official CLI so you keep full control.

Flags:
  --run           shell out to each detected client's CLI (prompts per command)
  --run --yes     shell out without prompting
  --print-config  print only the JSON snippet for manual paste (no detection)
  --skill         (not yet implemented) install Claude Code skill

**Flags**

| flag | type | default | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--print-config` | bool | `false` | print JSON snippet for manual paste (no detection output) |
| `--run` | bool | `false` | shell out to each detected client's CLI (prompts per command) |
| `--skill` | bool | `false` | install Claude Code skill (not yet implemented) |
| `--yes` | bool | `false` | skip per-command confirmation prompts (combine with --run) |

atomically claim a quest

**Usage**

```
guild quest accept QUEST_ID [flags]
```

| flag | type | default | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--json` | bool | `false` | emit structured JSON result instead of formatted text |
| `--owner` | string | `—` | claim owner (defaults to 'agent') |

| `--json` | bool | `false` | emit structured JSON result instead of formatted text |

## `guild quest brief`

write session-end handoff for the next agent

**Usage**

```
guild quest brief TEXT... [flags]
```

Session-end briefing for the next agent. Surfaced by next session's guild_session_start. Call before compact.

**Flags**

| flag | type | default | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--json` | bool | `false` | emit structured JSON result instead of formatted text |

## `guild quest campaign`

bulk-set campaign

**Usage**

```
guild quest campaign CAMPAIGN QUEST_IDS... [flags]
```

**Aliases:** epic

Set the campaign name on a batch of quests. Group related work for reporting. Also available as `quest epic` for backward compatibility.

**Flags**

| flag | type | default | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--json` | bool | `false` | emit structured JSON result instead of formatted text |

## `guild quest campfire`

save working state before compaction

**Usage**

```
guild quest campfire QUEST_ID [flags]
```

Save a structured working-state checkpoint (hypothesis, tried, next, token warning) before the context compacts. Distinct from journal — campfire is a restart beacon.

**Flags**

| flag | type | default | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--agent` | string | `—` | agent id (defaults to 'agent') |
| `--hypothesis` | string | `—` | current working hypothesis |
| `--json` | bool | `false` | emit structured JSON result instead of formatted text |
| `--next` | string | `—` | next step to try |
| `--token-warning` | bool | `false` | context window is running low |
| `--tried` | stringArray | `[]` | approach tried (repeatable) |

## `guild quest forfeit`

release a claim without completing

**Usage**

```
guild quest forfeit QUEST_ID [flags]
```

Release a claimed quest back to the queue. Use when blocked or ceding to another agent. Only acts on status=in_progress quests — refuses on done and no-ops on next.

**Flags**

| flag | type | default | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--json` | bool | `false` | emit structured JSON result instead of formatted text |
| `--note` | string | `—` | reason the claim is being released (optional) |

## `guild quest fulfill`

fulfill a quest (cascades unblock)

**Usage**

```
guild quest fulfill QUEST_ID [flags]
```

**Aliases:** clear

Fulfill a quest. Report is REQUIRED — commit hash, files, remaining issues. Cascades unblock dependent quests.

**Flags**

| flag | type | default | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--json` | bool | `false` | emit structured JSON result instead of formatted text |
| `--report` | string | `—` | completion report: commit hash, files, remaining issues |

## `guild quest guild`

per-project summary by campaign

**Usage**

```
guild quest guild [flags]
```

Per-project quest summary grouped by campaign: counts of next, in-progress, blocked, and done quests.

**Flags**

| flag | type | default | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--json` | bool | `false` | emit structured JSON result instead of formatted text |

## `guild quest init`

register this repo as a guild project

**Usage**

```
guild quest init
```

## `guild quest journal`

append a task-scoped journal note

**Usage**

```
guild quest journal QUEST_ID TEXT... [flags]
```

Task-scoped scratchpad. Dies when the quest clears. If it outlives the task, use lore_inscribe.

**Flags**

| flag | type | default | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--agent` | string | `—` | agent id (defaults to 'agent') |
| `--json` | bool | `false` | emit structured JSON result instead of formatted text |

## `guild quest list`

list open quests

**Usage**

```
guild quest list [flags]
```

All open tasks. Use after guild_session_start to spot parallelism — unshared files + no deps → spawn agents.

**Flags**

| flag | type | default | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--campaign` | string | `—` | filter by campaign |
| `--deps` | bool | `false` | include Depends and Blocks columns (CLI table) |
| `--epic` | string | `—` | filter by campaign (alias for --campaign) |
| `--files` | bool | `false` | include Files column (CLI table) |
| `--json` | bool | `false` | emit structured JSON result instead of formatted text |
| `--show-blocked` | bool | `false` | include blocked quests |
| `--status` | string | `—` | filter by status |

## `guild quest orders`

show quests assigned to an agent

**Usage**

```
guild quest orders [flags]
```

List quests currently assigned to a given agent owner. Use to see a teammate's queue or your own.

**Flags**

| flag | type | default | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--agent` | string | `—` | agent id to query (defaults to 'agent') |
| `--json` | bool | `false` | emit structured JSON result instead of formatted text |

## `guild quest post`

create a new quest

**Usage**

```
guild quest post SUBJECT... [flags]
```

Create a quest another agent can accept without human follow-up — well-specced quest = no human follow-up needed to execute it. Include rich WHY + HOW (rationale, approach, constraints) so a cold agent executes without re-deriving context from chat. Pass spec=... to atomically attach a kind=decision lore entry with full rationale (QUEST-63).

**Flags**

| flag | type | default | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--acceptance`, `-a` | stringArray | `[]` | acceptance criterion (repeatable) |
| `--campaign` | string | `—` | campaign name to group related quests |
| `--depends-on` | stringArray | `[]` | QUEST-ID this depends on (repeatable) |
| `--effort` | string | `—` | effort label |
| `--epic` | string | `—` | campaign name alias (--epic works as --campaign) |
| `--files` | stringArray | `[]` | file path (repeatable) |
| `--json` | bool | `false` | emit structured JSON result instead of formatted text |
| `--priority` | string | `—` | priority tag (P0\|P1\|P2\|P3) — default P2 |
| `--rework` | string | `—` | QUEST-X this is rework of |
| `--spec` | string | `—` | design rationale — atomically inscribes a kind=decision lore entry |

## `guild quest pulse`

quest quality dashboard: rework, churn, hot files

**Usage**

```
guild quest pulse [flags]
```

**Aliases:** health

Rework rate, churn rate, hot files, untested quests. Surfaces silent rework no one filed.

**Flags**

| flag | type | default | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--json` | bool | `false` | emit structured JSON result instead of formatted text |
| `--window-days` | int | `0` | window days for analysis (default 30) |

## `guild quest restore`

restore quest state from .guild/snapshot.json

**Usage**

```
guild quest restore [flags]
```

**Aliases:** import

**Flags**

| flag | type | default | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--path` | string | `—` | snapshot path (default: .guild/snapshot.json in project root) |

## `guild quest scroll`

full history: status + journal + timeline

**Usage**

```
guild quest scroll QUEST_ID [flags]
```

Full quest history: status, journal, timeline. Use to pick up a quest someone else was working on.

**Flags**

| flag | type | default | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--json` | bool | `false` | emit structured JSON result instead of formatted text |

## `guild quest search`

search quests by keyword or semantic paraphrase

**Usage**

```
guild quest search QUERY... [flags]
```

BM25+stopwords full-text search over quest subjects and spec notes. When quest vector coverage >= 90%, adds a semantic arm and RRF-fuses (k=60, same gate and fusion as lore_appraise). Returns up to 10 results. Replaces quest list --all | grep.

**Flags**

| flag | type | default | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--json` | bool | `false` | emit structured JSON result instead of formatted text |
| `--limit`, `-n` | int | `0` | max results (default 10) |

## `guild quest summon`

delegate a quest to a teammate agent

**Usage**

```
guild quest summon QUEST_ID [flags]
```

Reassign a quest to a named teammate agent. The quest's owner is updated; a [summon] event is recorded in the timeline.

**Flags**

| flag | type | default | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--agent` | string | `—` | caller agent id (defaults to 'agent') |
| `--json` | bool | `false` | emit structured JSON result instead of formatted text |
| `--to` | string | `—` | target agent id |

## `guild quest update`

modify a quest's spec after post

**Usage**

```
guild quest update QUEST_ID [flags]
```

Modify a quest's spec after post. Append lists via --files/-a/--depends-on/--blocks; replace via --replace-*; clear via --clear-*.

**Flags**

| flag | type | default | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--acceptance`, `-a` | stringArray | `[]` | append acceptance criterion (repeatable) |
| `--blocks` | stringArray | `[]` | append blocks (repeatable) |
| `--campaign` | string | `—` | new campaign name |
| `--clear-acceptance` | bool | `false` | clear acceptance list |
| `--clear-blocks` | bool | `false` | clear blocks list |
| `--clear-depends-on` | bool | `false` | clear depends_on list |
| `--clear-files` | bool | `false` | clear files list |
| `--depends-on` | stringArray | `[]` | append depends_on (repeatable) |
| `--effort` | string | `—` | new effort |
| `--epic` | string | `—` | new campaign name (alias for --campaign) |
| `--files` | stringArray | `[]` | append file (repeatable) |
| `--json` | bool | `false` | emit structured JSON result instead of formatted text |
| `--priority` | string | `—` | new priority |
| `--replace-acceptance` | stringArray | `[]` | replace acceptance entirely |
| `--replace-blocks` | stringArray | `[]` | replace blocks entirely |
| `--replace-depends-on` | stringArray | `[]` | replace depends_on entirely |
| `--replace-files` | stringArray | `[]` | replace files entirely |
| `--subject` | string | `—` | new subject |

## `guild status`

dashboard: last briefing, oath, top bounty, parallelism (alias of quest bounties)

**Usage**

```
guild status [flags]
```

Mid-session reorientation — shows the same snapshot the session-start
briefing prints, on demand.

Run this when you:
  - return to a repo after a break and need to catch up
  - want to see what the next agent would see on session_start
  - need to check oath drift or stale lore without re-running session_start

Same cost as quest bounties: one quest DB read + one lore DB read. No
new scans.

**Flags**

| flag | type | default | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--brief` | bool | `false` | show only the last briefing |

## `guild telemetry`

telemetry analytics (usage log, token estimates)

**Usage**

```
guild telemetry
```

## `guild telemetry tokens`

estimate token cost from usage.log response bytes

**Usage**

```
guild telemetry tokens [flags]
```

Reads ~/.guild/usage.log and estimates MCP response token cost.

Per-tool breakdown: call count, total resp_bytes, estimated tokens (4 chars/token), mean tokens/call.
Session aggregate: total tokens grouped by session (project + minute bucket).

Estimated at 4 chars/token — actual varies ~20%.

**Flags**

| flag | type | default | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--json` | bool | `false` | output as JSON |
| `--session` | string | `—` | filter to one session key (e.g. guild@2026-04-19T15:04); default: most recent session |

## `guild version`

print guild version information

**Usage**

```
guild version
```
